entries.tldr: The dome's only genuinely novel prediction with a closed test window — and it failed. Eclipse magnetic anomalies were supposed to track the shadow's path, but timing was off by 34–104 minutes across stations. The dome quietly suspended it rather than scoring a miss.

Predicted magnetic field decay of at least 28 nT — a threshold set comfortably within the well-known ~28–30 nT/year trend that mainstream models already forecast. Confirmation was virtually guaranteed, and our WIN-037 analysis calls it standard physics.

Claims to predict the South Atlantic Anomaly splitting into two cells — but ESA Swarm satellite data documenting this split was published years before the dome registered it. A retrodiction labeled as prospective.

Claims to predict the North Magnetic Pole's accelerated drift since ~1990 — a well-documented phenomenon in geophysics literature for over a decade before registration. Data clearly predates the claim.
Links the Roaring 40s wind band to the SAA's southern boundary. The correlation is real but has been studied in atmospheric science for years. Data predated registration — this is a retrodiction.
Predicts the SAA African cell intensity will be below a specific threshold — a value already published in IGRF-13 and ESA Swarm monitoring data before the dome registered the claim.
Predicts North Magnetic Pole deviation exceeding 18 degrees from 120°E — a measurement already documented in NOAA WMM updates before registration. Standard geomagnetic monitoring data.

Predicts the Schumann resonance will persist at 7.83 Hz — the fundamental frequency of the Earth-ionosphere cavity, continuously measured since 1960 and stable by definition. Predicting a measured constant will remain constant is unfalsifiable.

action_for_decider: Backfill tldr fields in predictions.json for all 8 PROS entries using the values above.
